content: Adaptive active memory compression for long-term agent retention can be dynamically gated by the agent's current working memory capacity limit during reasoning, creating a "just-in-time summarization" system that only compresses older memories when working memory is at risk of overflow rather than compressing on a fixed schedule.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
